name: California Adventist
description_short: Member-owned federal credit union serving Seventh-day Adventist communities since 1952, offering personal and business banking with $47M+ in assets.
description_long:
California Adventist Federal Credit Union (CAFCU) was established in 1952 as the country's first Seventh-day Adventist credit union. Operating as a not-for-profit, member-owned institution regulated by the NCUA, CAFCU has built a 73-year track record serving Adventist church members, employees of Adventist organizations, and referred community members across California.

CAFCU offers a comprehensive range of financial products including personal checking and savings accounts, debit cards, online and mobile banking, business checking accounts, loans and lines of credit, credit cards with loyalty rewards, CDs, and retirement investment services. The credit union maintains two physical branch locations in Glendale and Simi Valley, both housed within Adventist Health facilities, with staffed hours Monday-Thursday 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM and Friday 8:00 AM to 12:30 PM.

The institution distinguishes itself through personalized service, emphasis on member relationships, competitive rates on auto loans and savings products, and a long institutional commitment to helping members improve creditworthiness over time. Member testimonials highlight accessibility for those with credit challenges, competitive auto loan rates, and knowledgeable staff. The credit union has undergone recent management transitions but maintains service consistency according to member feedback.

CAFCU's primary limitation is membership eligibility—it primarily serves Seventh-day Adventist church members and employees of Adventist organizations, with limited availability to non-Adventist referrals. As a smaller credit union with $47M in assets and only two physical branches, it may have more limited service capacity than larger financial institutions. Specific APR ranges, current rate offerings, and detailed fee schedules are not published on the website.
services: Personal savings accounts and share accounts
Business loans and lines of credit
Credit cards with rewards and low-interest options
pros: First Seventh-day Adventist credit union in the country with 73 years of established history and member trust
Competitive auto loan rates specifically noted by multiple members as a key strength
Personalized service model with staff trained to work with members on credit improvement and financial goals
NCUA insurance protection for member deposits up to $250,000
Mobile and online banking available for convenient account access
Low-interest loyalty Visa card option for qualified members
Willingness to work with members who have imperfect credit histories, as documented in member testimonials
cons: Membership restricted primarily to Seventh-day Adventist community members and Adventist organization employees, limiting accessibility
Only two physical branch locations (Glendale and Simi Valley), both in Southern California, with no indication of branch expansion
Website does not publish specific interest rates, APRs, or fee schedules for any products, making rate comparison impossible
Limited asset base ($47M) compared to larger credit unions may restrict loan amounts or product availability
Website lacks information on business lending limits, CD terms, investment options specifics, and retirement account offerings
best_for: Seventh-day Adventist church members seeking personalized banking with community connection
Small business owners affiliated with Adventist organizations looking for business checking and lending
Individuals with fair credit looking to improve credit history with a lender willing to work with them
Southern California residents in the Glendale or Simi Valley areas needing local branch access
diagnosis: CAFCU is best for Seventh-day Adventist community members and Adventist organization employees in Southern California who value personalized service and community connection alongside competitive rates. The primary caveat is restrictive membership eligibility and limited geographic footprint; non-Adventist individuals should confirm membership eligibility before pursuing accounts, and those outside Southern California have no physical branch access.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: California Adventist — Glendale, CA Credit Union | CreditDoc
meta_description: California Adventist — NCUA-insured credit union in Glendale, CA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
